questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can finish a job in $12$ days and B in $6$ days. Working together, they finish it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the per-day work rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{6}=\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{2}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ days.

**Answer.** $4$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The perimeter of a square of side $9$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perimeter of a square $=4\times\text{side}$.

**Working.** $4\times 9 = 36$.

**Answer.** $36$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: 'By the time the rescue team arrived, the trapped miners ____ for nearly ten hours.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An action continuing up to a point in the past uses the past perfect continuous tense.

**Working.** The reference point ('the team arrived') is in the past, and the waiting was ongoing before it. This requires 'had been + verb-ing'.

**Answer.** had been waiting.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert $72$ km/h into m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply km/h by $\tfrac{5}{18}$ to get m/s.

**Working.** $72\times\dfrac{5}{18}=20$ m/s.

**Answer.** $20$ m/s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article costing $200$ is sold for $250$. The profit percentage is _____ % .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit\% $=\dfrac{\text{SP}-\text{CP}}{\text{CP}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{250-200}{200}\times 100 = 25\%$$

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The number of distinct ways to arrange all the letters of the word 'MINE' is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** With all letters distinct, arrangements $=n!$.

**Working.** $4!=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many triangles are there in the square with one diagonal drawn (shown)?

figure.caption: A square with a single diagonal, splitting it into two triangles.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='15' width='100' height='100' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='15' x2='115' y2='115' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** One diagonal cuts the square into two equal triangles.

**Working.** Each half is a triangle, so there are $2$.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A card is drawn at random from a standard $52$-card deck. The probability that it is an ace is _____ (round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** There are $4$ aces in $52$ cards.

**Working.** $\dfrac{4}{52}=\dfrac{1}{13}\approx 0.08$.

**Answer.** $\approx 0.08$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The net shown is folded into a cube. Which face is opposite face 2?

figure.caption: A cube net: a horizontal row of faces 1, 2, 3, 4 with face 5 above face 2 and face 6 below face 2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)'><rect x='20' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='140' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='30' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='110' width='40' height='40'/></g><g font-size='16' fill='#334155'><text x='36' y='95'>1</text><text x='76' y='95'>2</text><text x='116' y='95'>3</text><text x='156' y='95'>4</text><text x='76' y='55'>5</text><text x='76' y='135'>6</text></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a straight row of four faces, faces two apart are opposite.

**Working.** With 2 as the front, 1 is left, 3 is right and 4 is back; front (2) is opposite back (4).

**Answer.** 4.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Triangle is to $3$ sides as the pentagon shown is to

figure.caption: A regular pentagon.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='60,15 110,52 91,108 29,108 10,52' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The analogy maps a polygon to its number of sides.

**Working.** A pentagon has $5$ sides.

**Answer.** 5 sides.

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $10\,\text{t}$ of methane (GWP$_{100}=28$). Its carbon-dioxide-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 280</text><rect x="190" y="145.71428571428572" width="46" height="4.285714285714286" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="141.71428571428572" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 10</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄ → CO₂-e (t)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Methane converted to CO₂-equivalent via GWP.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e $=$ mass $\times$ GWP.

**Working.**
$$10\times28=280.$$

**Answer.** $280\,\text{t CO}_2\text{-e}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A higher value of the Shannon–Wiener index $H'$ indicates
questions.solution:
**Concept.** H' increases with richness and evenness.

**Working.**
Greater diversity.

**Answer.** Greater diversity

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Which statement about the EIA process in India is correct?
options: Public consultation is a mandatory stage for Category A projects
EIA is optional for all projects
Scoping precedes the identification of the project

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 90" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="8" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="38" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Screening</text><path d="M68 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="78" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="108" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Scoping</text><path d="M138 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="148" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="178" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Impact</text><text x="178" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">prediction</text><path d="M208 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="218" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="248" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">EMP</text><path d="M278 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="288" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="318" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Review &</text><text x="318" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">clearance</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Public consultation (public hearing + written responses) is a statutory stage for most Category A and B1 projects under EIA Notification 2006.

**Working.**
Public hearing is mandatory.

**Answer.** Public consultation is mandatory

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $10$ identical sources each $80\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log10=90.$$

**Answer.** $90\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{120}{500}\right)\times100=76\%.$$

**Answer.** $76\%$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $3$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 8000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$8000\times0.1^{2}=80.$$

**Answer.** $80$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A tannery's discharge requires $₹4.5$ lakh of effluent-treatment (abatement) and additionally causes $₹2.8$ lakh of measurable downstream crop damage. Under the polluter-pays principle, the amount (in $₹$ lakh) the tannery must internalise is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The polluter-pays principle internalises the full externality: the polluter bears abatement **plus** damage costs (not just one of them).

**Working.**
$$4.5+2.8=7.3\text{ lakh}.$$

**Answer.** $₹7.3$ lakh

questions.stem: For the landfill shown, read the usable air-space and the annual fill rate from the diagram. Its design life is _____ years.

**Concept.** Life = air-space/annual fill, both read from the figure.

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a reactor, $30\,\text{mg/L}$ of biomass is produced while $120\,\text{mg/L}$ of substrate is consumed. The observed yield $Y_{X/S}$ is _____ mg/mg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Y_{X/S}=\dfrac{\Delta X}{\Delta S}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{30}{120}=0.25.$$

**Answer.** $0.25$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $50\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1000(1-50/100)=500.$$

**Answer.** $500\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">7</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$7+1.5=8.5.$$

**Answer.** $8.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=6.25\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $6.25\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="134" width="50" height="16" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="130" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">600</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{80}{600}\right)\times100=86.7\%.$$

**Answer.** $86.7\%$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $18\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 21</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$18\times21=378.$$

**Answer.** $378$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.05\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.05}=13.86.$$

**Answer.** $13.86\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.231\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A direct consequence of eutrophication in a water body is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Algal blooms die and decompose, consuming DO and raising BOD.

**Working.**
DO depletion.

**Answer.** Oxygen depletion

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the rectangular settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (in MLD) and the plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 8 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 400 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $SOR=\dfrac{Q}{A}$ (1 MLD = 1000 m³/day), with $Q$ and $A$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{8\times1000}{400}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m/day}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $2$ identical sources each $85\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log2=88.01.$$

**Answer.** $88.01\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Influent BOD $250$, effluent $25\,\text{mg/L}$. Removal efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 250</text><rect x="190" y="138" width="46" height="12" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="134" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 25</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">BOD (mg/L)</text></svg>
figure.caption: BOD: influent vs. effluent (mg/L).

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: An abatement benefit arrives as the single future cash flow shown in the diagram. At a discount rate of $8\%$, its present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,00,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 5</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Present worth $=\dfrac{F}{(1+i)^n}$, with $F$ and $n$ read from the cash-flow diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100000}{(1+0.08)^{5}}=68058.$$

**Answer.** $₹68058$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">4</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">0.5</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$100+4\times28+0.5\times265=344.5.$$

**Answer.** $344.5$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste $1000\,\text{kg}$ yields $0.2\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$ gas. Total gas is _____ m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas = mass × yield.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.2=200.$$

**Answer.** $200$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.08\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $13$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 1000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000e^{0.08\times13}=2829.$$

**Answer.** $2829$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.2\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $4$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="110" width="60" height="40" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 80</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$80e^{-0.2\times4}=35.95.$$

**Answer.** $35.95$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $8\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$ of flue gas. Reading the collecting area $A$ and the drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its Deutsch–Anderson collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 300 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Deutsch–Anderson: $\eta=1-e^{-wA/Q}$, with $A$ and $w$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-e^{-0.12\times300/8}\right)\times100=98.89\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.89\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.5</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times100}{50+100}=0.333.$$

**Answer.** $0.333$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">6</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$6+1=7.$$

**Answer.** $7\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.05\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $8$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 1000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000e^{0.05\times8}=1492.$$

**Answer.** $1492$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=45\%$, $H=5\%$, $O=40\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 15.2</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(45)+1419(5-40/8)=15165.$$

**Answer.** $15165\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A water contains $40\,\text{mg/L}$ of $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ and $12\,\text{mg/L}$ of $\text{Mg}^{2+}$. The total hardness as $\text{CaCO}_3$ is _____ mg/L. (Eq. wt: Ca 20, Mg 12.15, CaCO₃ 50)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="160" x2="305" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="14" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="240" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant added →</text><line x1="38" y1="89" x2="305" y2="89" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/><text x="42" y="86" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">pH 7</text><path d="M38 150 C120 146 160 140 168 89 C176 38 230 32 305 28" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="168" cy="89" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence pt</text></svg>
figure.caption: EDTA titration is used to measure total hardness.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to $\text{CaCO}_3$: $\text{mg/L}\times\dfrac{50}{\text{eq.wt}}$.

**Working.**
$$40\times\dfrac{50}{20}+12\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=149.4.$$

**Answer.** $149.4\,\text{mg/L as CaCO}_3$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $8$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="75" width="60" height="75" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 150</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$150e^{-0.1\times8}=67.4.$$

**Answer.** $67.4$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="126" width="50" height="24" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="122" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">400</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{80}{400}\right)\times100=80\%.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $50\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.2\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">15</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.2\times15}{50}=0.06.$$

**Answer.** $0.06\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 300 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.5\,\text{m}}{300\,\text{s}}\times1000=8.33\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $8.33\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Two sources $70\,\text{dB}$ and $73\,\text{dB}$ at a point. Combined level is _____ dB.

questions.stem: $L_0=200\,\text{mg/L}$, $k=0.2\,\text{d}^{-1}$ (base e). BOD exerted in $6$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.caption: Oxygen-sag curve: critical deficit D_c at critical time t_c.

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 2</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{2}}=102881.$$

**Answer.** $₹102881$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $3$ identical sources each $70\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$70+10\log3=74.77.$$

**Answer.** $74.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="114" width="50" height="36" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="110" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">24</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80\times\dfrac{50}{20}+24\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=298.8.$$

**Answer.** $298.8$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=40\%$, $H=7\%$, $O=35\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 17.2</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(7-35/8)=17205.$$

**Answer.** $17205\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A river has DO saturation $9.2\,\text{mg/L}$. From the oxygen-sag curve shown, read the critical deficit $D_c$. The minimum dissolved oxygen in the stream is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 200" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="30" x2="34" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="175" x2="330" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="8" y="40" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">DO</text><text x="305" y="190" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">distance/time →</text><line x1="34" y1="45" x2="330" y2="45" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="250" y="42" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">saturation DO</text><path d="M34 60 Q120 175 175 150 Q260 110 330 70" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="175" y1="45" x2="175" y2="150" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="175" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="180" y="120" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">D_c = 2.5 mg/L</text><text x="150" y="170" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">t_c = 2.5 d</text></svg>
figure.caption: Critical deficit D_c (and t_c) marked on the sag curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Minimum DO = DO_sat − D_c, with D_c read from the sag curve.

**Working.**
$$9.2-2.5=6.7.$$

**Answer.** $6.7\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.05\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $15$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 500</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$500e^{0.05\times15}=1059.$$

**Answer.** $1059$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A refrigerant recovery releases $2\,\text{t}$ of CFC-11 (ODP $=1.0$) and $5\,\text{t}$ of HCFC-22 (ODP $=0.055$). The total release expressed as CFC-11-equivalent (ODP-weighted) is _____ t.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ODP-weighted release $=\sum(\text{mass}\times\text{ODP})$ — the basis on which the Montreal Protocol controls ozone-depleting substances.

**Working.**
$$2(1.0)+5(0.055)=2.275\,\text{t}.$$

**Answer.** $2.275\,\text{t CFC-11-eq}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $12\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 265</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$12\times265=3180.$$

**Answer.** $3180$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 500 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\text{depth}\div\text{time}$, both read from the trajectory.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2\,\text{m}}{500\,\text{s}}\times1000=4\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $4\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 300 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.08 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.08\times300/6})\times100=98.17\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.17\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $6\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.06)^{2}}=44500.$$

**Answer.** $₹44500$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The main purpose of maintaining ~850–1100 °C with adequate residence time in an MSW incinerator is to
options: ensure complete combustion and destroy dioxins/pathogens
reduce the need for air pollution control
questions.solution:
**Concept.** High temperature + 2 s residence ensures complete burnout and dioxin destruction.

**Working.**
Complete combustion.

**Answer.** Complete combustion

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=80\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 60</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times80}{60+80}=0.171.$$

**Answer.** $0.171$
